area: Broker / transactional commit queue / parallel closeout
finding: Plan 3.1 dogfood showed that ATM can coordinate task claims, leases, broker conflicts, runner-sync admission, and task closeout, but it does not yet provide a broker-managed transactional stage/commit queue. When Claude completed ATM-GOV-0255, release/build artifacts had to be staged and committed through a manually supervised framework-mode claim and native git fallback. During that window, another captain could not safely write even an unrelated backlog item because the shared git index could mix two actors' staged files. ATM also left runner-sync receipt files as manual-review residue without an automatic archival or cleanup path.
expectedBehavior: Agents should submit scoped commit requests to ATM instead of manually sharing git add/git commit. Each request should carry actor id, task id or framework-temp id, allowed files, sealed-source SHA, evidence references, commit message, and artifact surface. ATM should serialize requests, acquire queue-head, stage only the request-owned files, commit with governed trailers, clean the index, release the queue, and return a verifiable result. Post-close release artifact publication and runner-sync receipts should have first-class playbooks or recoveryCommand output so external captains can finish without human step-by-step intervention.
evidenceOrRepro: ATM-GOV-0255 on 2026-07-22: delivery commit 6a89ed0d2f0c6f61ee0cb6d5dc7f27026716d5f3, close bundle commits 9ad17b21fb05ef401a3d1c47bd6dd3de62fce84d and ad15994d834356ce69b616e520189b359b042fcd, release artifact commit 947c6b7839bb2136ee024a476ade132632d99726. The external captain needed human pre-approval for the exact framework-mode claim over packages/cli/dist, release/atm-onefile/atm.mjs, release/atm-onefile/release-manifest.json, and release/atm-root-drop before the classifier allowed the release commit. Before that commit completed, `node atm.mjs next --prompt "請按照你的建議進行下一步" --json` reported L3 shared-index risk with 17 staged release/build files. After release, `node atm.mjs next --prompt "Plan3.1 record backlog for broker-managed transactional commit stage queue and runner receipt residue after ATM-GOV-0255 release artifact hygiene" --json` still reported 7 unowned dirty WIP receipt/import residue files and required a separate framework-temp claim for this backlog write.
followUp: Create a Plan 3.1 insertion card or Plan 3.2 task for `atm commit broker submit/status/wait` or equivalent transactional commit queue. Add regressions that run two actors with disjoint commit requests in one worktree, verify queue ordering and index isolation, publish post-close runner-sync release artifacts through the same queue, and assert zero manual captain intervention, zero foreign staged files, and no manual-review runner receipt residue.
